channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: How to register, update voter card on INEC’s online self-service portal
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Learn how to register, update, or transfer your INEC voter card using the new online self-service portal. Get your PVC easily ahead of elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/how-to-register-update-voter-card-on-inecs-online-self-service-portal/

item.title: Germany recorded 32% spike in deaths during June heatwave
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Germany recorded a 32% spike in deaths during the June heatwave, with 23,900 fatalities. Learn more about the impact of the Germany heatwave deaths.
Read More: https://punchng.com/germany-recorded-32-spike-in-deaths-during-june-heatwave/

item.title: 2027: Pantami raises security concerns over Gombe tour, alleges intimidation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Minister Isa Ali Pantami alleges intimidation and security threats during his thank-you tour across Gombe State ahead of the 2027 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-pantami-raises-security-concerns-over-gombe-tour-alleges-intimidation/

item.title: Bring Michael’s body home, slain Oyo teacher’s family begs Tinubu, Makinde
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The family of Michael Oyedokun, a teacher slain during an Oyo abduction, appeals to President Tinubu and Governor Makinde to recover his body for burial.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bring-michaels-body-home-slain-oyo-teachers-family-begs-tinubu-makinde/

item.title: My husband worked for me for 11 months after losing his pilot job – Businesswoman
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian businesswoman Bunmi George shares how her husband worked for her company and earned a salary for 11 months after losing his pilot job, strengtheni
Read More: https://punchng.com/my-husband-worked-for-me-for-11-months-after-losing-his-pilot-job-businesswoman/

item.title: Brighton complete club-record deal for Spurs' Vuskovic
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Brighton have completed the club-record signing of Luka Vuskovic from Spurs.

item.title: Is Flower the 'standout' candidate to replace McCullum?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Nasser Hussain and Michael Atherton discuss who they think should replace Brendon McCullum as the England Test head coach.

item.title: Soyinka deserves greater national honour at 92, Moghalu hails Nobel Laureate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kingsley Moghalu advocates for greater national honour for Nobel Laureate Wole Soyinka at 92, praising his enduring contributions to Nigeria and the world.
Read More: https://punchng.com/soyinka-deserves-greater-national-honour-at-92-moghalu-hails-nobel-laureate/

item.title: C’River gov orders urgent repair of failed road section
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State Governor Bassey Otu has ordered urgent repairs on the failed sections of the Calabar-Odukpani Road to ensure commuter safety and convenie
Read More: https://punchng.com/criver-gov-orders-urgent-repair-of-failed-road-section/

item.title: Eno approves ₦1.3bn bursary, raises A’Ibom students’ grant to ₦50,000
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Akwa Ibom Governor Umo Eno approves ₦1.3bn bursary, increasing student grants to ₦50,000. This initiative aims to ease financial burdens and boost educatio
Read More: https://punchng.com/eno-approves-%e2%82%a61-3bn-bursary-raises-aibom-students-grant-to-%e2%82%a650000/

item.title: Xenophobia: Final batch of 315 Nigerians to return from S’Africa Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The final batch of 315 Nigerians is set to return from South Africa on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, following recent xenophobic attacks.
Read More: https://punchng.com/xenophobia-final-batch-of-315-nigerians-to-return-from-safrica-wednesday/

item.title: England vs Argentina tactical dossier: How Tuchel’s side can reach World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Here we go. England face Argentina in the World Cup semi-finals on Wednesday - a fixture loaded with history, jeopardy and one final obstacle between Thomas Tuchel’s side and the World Cup final. It may be the biggest England game of a generation, but where could it be won or lost?

item.title: SDP presidential candidate hails Soyinka’s enduring legacy at 92
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
SDP presidential candidate, Prince Adewole Adebayo, celebrates Nobel laureate Wole Soyinka on his 92nd birthday, praising his enduring legacy of courage an
Read More: https://punchng.com/sdp-presidential-candidate-hails-soyinkas-enduring-legacy-at-92/

item.title: England bat after winning toss vs India in first ODI at Edgbaston LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Four Decades After Maradona Stunned England, Messi Gets His Chance
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The man who emulated Diego Maradona by inspiring Argentina to World Cup glory in Qatar four years ago will now hope to leave a similar mark on England as his legendary predecessor. The post Four Decades After Maradona Stunned England, Messi Gets His Chance appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Japan Approves New Social Media Rules For Elections
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Japan has enacted new rules governing the use of social media during elections, requiring artificial intelligence-generated images and videos to be clearly labelled and prohibiting the spread of false or misleading information about candidates, the country’s internal affairs minister announced

item.title: World Cup 2026: Referee with VAR controversy to officiate England vs Argentina
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: FIFA has announced that American referee Ismail Elfath will officiate the highly anticipated 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final clash between England and Argentina.

item.title: US strikes Iran, vows to reimpose naval blockade
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The United States launched on Tuesday a fresh wave of strikes on Iran and President Donald Trump vowed to reimpose a naval blockade, prompting Tehran to respond with attacks on countries in the region. The post US strikes Iran, vows to reimpose naval blockade appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: UK: Migrants shouldn’t stay indefinitely – Kemi Badenoch
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: UK Conservative Party leader Kemi Badenoch has said that people who enter Britain on temporary work visas should not automatically be granted the right to remain in the country permanently, urging the Labour government to maintain its proposed reforms that extend the qualifying period for indefinite

item.title: FEATURE… When Treatment Becomes Tragedy: The growing toll of medical negligence in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: At just 28, Idris Buko still remembers the injection that changed his life forever. Growing up in Boriya community, Baruten Local Government Area of Kwara State, North Central Nigeria, he was healthy just like other children until a wrong injection treatment for malaria left him with a physical defo

item.title: Nigeria & Brighton star beats Chelsea & Man United GKs to top WSL rankings
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Brighton goalkeeper Chiamaka Nnadozie has finished her first season in England on top with the crown for the division's save queen, Soccernet.ng reports. The Super Falcons shot-stopper has been confirmed as the goalkeeper with the highest save percentage in the 2025/2026 Barclays Women's Super Leagu

item.title: BUK, European Space Agency Train Journalists in Earth observation for climate reporting
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Journalists and media practitioners have been urged to adopt earth observation technologies to improve the quality of climate change and environmental reporting in Nigeria. The charge was given yesterday at a one-day capacity-building workshop organised by Bayero University, Kano(BUK), in collaborat

item.title: Indian Activist’s Health Worsens in Hunger Strike for Students
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The health of a well-known Indian activist is deteriorating more than two weeks into his hunger strike over exam paper leaks, putting pressure on the government to respond to one of the country’s most prominent youth-driven protests in years.

item.title: Fed Hike Bets Mount Ahead of CPI Data, Warsh Hearing
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bond traders ramped up bets for a July interest-rate hike ahead of US inflation data and an appearance by the head of the Federal Reserve that stand to reinforce the need for action. Bloomberg's Ven Ram reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: We handed electronic materials recovered from terrorists to US forces – Nigerian Military
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian military has dismissed claims by a senior United States official that American forces seized a large cache of electronic materials from terrorists during an operation in Nigeria and flew the materials out of the country. Refuting the claims, Director of Defence Information, Brig-Gen Sam

item.title: Deutsche Bank May Win Russia-Sanctions Lawsuit Against Linde
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG may win a legal battle in a dispute with a corporate client over who has to pick up the tap for losses linked to sanctions on Russia, according to a German court.

item.title: College Urges FG, States To Adopt Consultant Pharmacist Cadre
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The West African Postgraduate College of Pharmacists (WAPCP), Nigeria Chapter, has called on the federal and state governments to implement the consultant pharmacist cadre across the country. It described the move as critical to improving patient care, medication safety and the overall quality of he

item.description: Smartphone durability is often communicated through specification sheets and laboratory certifications. item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The court gave lawyers 30 days before their scheduled hearing dates to complete the uploading of the required documents. The post Supreme Court asks lawyers to upload appeal documents under new electronic filing system appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Social Democratic Party (SDP), Prince Adewole Adebayo, has congratulated Nobel Laureate, Prof. Wole Soyinka, on his 92nd birthday, describing him as a living legend whose life embodies courage, integrity and an unwavering commitment to justice. In a statement

item.title: All On opens applications for 2026 Off-Grid Energy Challenge, offers up to $1m in funding
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: All On, Nigeria's off-grid energy access impact investor, has launched the 2026 All On Off-Grid Energy Challenge, inviting Nigerian clean energy companies to compete for funding of up to $1 million to scale innovative solutions aimed at expanding energy access across the country.

item.title: Muslim Group Hails Rescue Of Oyo Pupils, Calls For Stronger Security
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Organisation of Tadhamunul Muslimeen (OTM) has welcomed the rescue of 44 abducted pupils, teachers and school officials in Oyo State, describing the development as a major relief for affected families and communities. In a statement issued yesterday by its national secretary, Alhaji Jamiu Aleem,

item.title: NIPR Inducts 70 Nigeria Customs Public Relations Officers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Institute of Public Relations (NIPR) has inducted 70 Public Relations officers of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), marking the Service’s first mass induction into the Institute since 2013. The induction ceremony held recently at the Nigeria Customs Service Headquarters in Maitam

item.title: US Strikes Iran For Third Day, Will Reimpose Blockade
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The US military said its five-hour mission hit targets across Iran including coastal Bushehr and Bandar Abbas to "degrade Iran's ability to attack commercial shipping". The post US Strikes Iran For Third Day, Will Reimpose Blockade appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: PBOC Boosts Liquidity to Smooth Tax Payments, Debt Issuance
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s central bank said it’s boosting longer-term liquidity injections through outright reverse repurchase agreements, a move expected to help companies and individuals handle upcoming tax payments and to sustain demand for debt issuance.

item.title: Final Evacuation Flight From South Africa to Bring Home 315 Nigerians Wednesday
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has announced that the fifth and final evacuation flight for Nigerians stranded in South Africa will arrive in Lagos on Wednesday, bringing home 315 returnees. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs disclosed this in a statement on Tuesday through its spokesperson, Kimiebi Imomotimi

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Ed Miliband Seen as Least Appealing UK Chancellor: Markets Pulse
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Andy Burnham is viewed as bearish for UK assets, with former Labour Party leader Ed Miliband seen as the least market-friendly pick for Chancellor, according to Bloomberg's latest Markets Pulse Survey. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Gilgit-Baltistan: Pakistan's tourist jewel tested by development and climate change
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In just a few decades, the Hunza Valley in Gilgit-Baltistan, a remote mountainous region of northern Pakistan, has undergone a dramatic transformation. Expanded access to education, improved healthcare and strong local development have reshaped the area, in a model of progress often linked to founda

item.description: Ukrainian drones hit several Russian vessels in the Sea of ​Azov ‌overnight on Tuesday, Kyiv's ‌drone forces commander said, as the Kremlin denied that repeated Ukrainian attacks on the major export route would impact Russia's export capacity.

item.title: Police investigate Jayden Adam's death after he was found in CapeTown property
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The South African international football player Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25. He was in all three ​of his side's group stage games in the World Cup. No cause of death has been given. A police investigation is underway in Cape Town. Details by Nicholas Rushworth.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has concluded arrangements for the final evacuation of stranded Nigerians from South Africa, with 315 citizens expected to arrive in Lagos on Wednesday aboard an Air Peace flight, bringing to a close the emergency repatriation exercise launched in response to recent xenophobic

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: After fifteen years of relative decline, the inexorably falling Japanese yen has made Tokyo one of the world’s cheapest major cities

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Equities traders seem unperturbed by the dizzying array of key events on the calendar Tuesday, creating a precarious setup with the stock market hovering near a record high.

item.title: Anelka: Why I Was Expelled From France Squad At World Cup 2010
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Arsenal striker Nicolas Anelka has reflected on his infamous 18-match ban, which he received after a furious dressing-room altercation with France manager Raymond Domenech during the 2010 World Cup in South Africa. Domenech allegedly substituted the striker at halftime against Mexico, leading

item.title: Onanuga faults Makinde’s UN probe call over Oyo abduction
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Presidency has criticised Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde's call for a United Nations-backed investigation into the abduction and rescue of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area, describing the demand as unnecessary and politically motivated. The post Onanuga faults Makinde’s

item.title: India’s Birla Taps MUFG for $1.5 Billion to Fund Shell Asset Buy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A unit of billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla’s conglomerate has lined up around $1.5 billion of acquisition financing from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. to fund its purchase of Shell Plc’s renewable energy assets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: A winner and true friend - ex-Wimbledon champion Bartoli on Deschamps
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former Wimbledon champion Marion Bartoli speaks about her friendship with France boss Didier Deschamps.

item.title: BofA Warns Stock Bulls to Consider Curbing Aggressive Buying
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Global investors buying stocks aggressively should consider reducing exposure, according to Bank of America Corp.’s fund manager survey.

item.title: Why We Remember Scenes More Than Movies
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Ask any Nigerian film lover to name their favourite? Nollywood classic. They will rarely start by explaining the three-act structure or the final narrative resolution. Instead, they will describe a tense dinner table confrontation or a legendary insult delivered with precise comedic timing. This hyp

item.title: ‘Impeccably designed’: You can rent Orlando Bloom’s former Beverly Hills estate for just $31,000 a month
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Actor Orlando Bloom’s former Beverly Hills mansion is being offered up as a luxury rental, with a hefty asking price of $31,000 a month—with many of the Hollywood star’s custom upgrades still found throughout.

item.title: VIDEO: Why I want UN, other international bodies to investigate Oyo abduction — Makinde
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, has explained why he called on the United Nations and other international human rights and accountability bodies to investigate the abduction of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of the state. The post VIDEO: Why I want UN, other international bodi

item.title: England vs Argentina: Agbonlahor, Bent hand Tuchel names of players to play in attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former England internationals, Gabby Agbonlahor and Darren Bent, have handed manager, Thomas Tuchel the names of players to feature in the Three Lions’ attacking line against Argentina in the World Cup semi-final fixture on Wednesday. Speaking on talkSPORT, Agbonlahor urged Tuchel to star

item.title: ‘God is punishing the politicians’: anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Threat of social unrest rises as public indignation at lack of disaster aid comes on top of fallout from US military intervention• A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit VenezuelaPublic anger at what many perceive as the Venezuelan government’s botched response

item.title: Navy Arrests Two, Recover Vandalised Oil Pipeline In Imo
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Sylvester Idowu in Warri  Operatives of the Naval Base Oguta have arrested a 57-year-old retired Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP) identified as Alfred Kwosa for alleged vandalism of oil pipeline

item.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

item.title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature. The order blocks new env
